name: plain-app
description: 🔥 PlainApp is an open-source app that lets you securely manage your phone from a web browser. Access files, media, contacts, SMS, calls, and more through a simple, easy-to-use interface on your desktop.
readme_summary: PlainApp is an open-source Android application that provides a self-hosted, web-based interface for managing your phone's files, media, contacts, SMS, and more from any device on the same network. It prioritizes privacy and security with local-only operation and end-to-end encryption.
key_features: Built-in tools: Markdown notes, RSS reader, media player, P2P chat
primary_use_case: Securely managing and accessing an Android phone's data and features from a web browser on the same local network, without relying on cloud services.
cover_image_prompt: A vintage, ornate wooden desk acting as a central hub. On the desk, a single, glowing Android phone is connected by a subtle, shimmering data stream to an open laptop displaying a clean, organized dashboard interface. The data stream visually represents files, photos, and messages flowing seamlessly between the phone and the laptop. The background is a softly lit, cozy study, emphasizing the local and private nature of the connection. Small, almost imperceptible digital particles emanate from the data stream, hinting at the underlying technology. The overall mood is one of secure, effortless control and accessibility. The art style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a warm, inviting color palette and clear, defined elements.

name: deb-get
description: apt-get for .debs published via GitHub or direct download 📦
readme_summary: deb-get is a command-line tool that extends apt-get functionality to manage .deb packages published via GitHub releases or direct downloads. It simplifies the installation, upgrade, and removal of software not available in standard Ubuntu/Debian repositories.
key_features: Install .deb packages from GitHub releases or direct download URLs
Update and upgrade packages installed via deb-get
Support for GitHub Personal Access Tokens for API authorization
Works on Debian, Ubuntu, and derivatives
primary_use_case: Managing .deb packages from third-party sources that are not available in official repositories, simplifying software installation and updates on Debian-based systems.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly robot librarian is organizing books on a shelf. Each book represents a .deb package. The robot is taking books from a digital cloud labeled 'GitHub Releases' and a direct download icon, and placing them neatly onto a shelf labeled 'My System'. Some books have glowing 'update' arrows. Subtle UI elements showing package names and versions are visible on the books. The background is a clean, modern library with soft, inviting lighting. The style is a 3D isometric illustration with clear, friendly character design and a vibrant color palette, emphasizing ease of use and organization.

name: netwatch
description: Real-time network diagnostics in your terminal. One command, zero config, instant visibility.
readme_summary: NetWatch is a real-time network diagnostics tool for the terminal, offering instant visibility into network interfaces, connections, and packet data. It provides both high-level overviews and deep packet inspection capabilities, along with a flight recorder feature to capture transient network events.
key_features: Connection monitoring with process names and PIDs
Gateway and DNS health checks with latency heatmap
Flight recorder for transient event capture
Incident bundle export (summary, connections, health, bandwidth, DNS, alerts, packets)
primary_use_case: Real-time network monitoring and troubleshooting directly within the terminal, providing both a broad overview and detailed packet-level insights.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant network diagnostician, depicted as a modern-day lighthouse keeper, standing in a dimly lit control room filled with glowing screens. The lighthouse beam cuts through a stormy sea, illuminating a complex network of ships (representing data packets) and submarines (representing connections). One screen shows a live feed of network traffic visualized as a dynamic, flowing river, while another displays a 'flight recorder' spooling out a timeline of past events. Subtle digital particles and glowing lines connect the screens and the lighthouse beam, symbolizing real-time data flow. The NetWatch logo is subtly integrated into the lighthouse's base. The overall mood is one of intense focus and control amidst chaos, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a cool, blue-toned color palette and sharp, clear details.

name: agents
description: Trade autonomously on Polymarket using AI Agents
readme_summary: Polymarket Agents is a Python-based developer framework for building AI agents that can autonomously trade on the Polymarket prediction market platform. It provides tools for integrating with the Polymarket API, sourcing data, and utilizing LLMs for market predictions and trade execution.
key_features: AI agent utilities for prediction markets
Data sourcing from betting services, news providers, and web search
Comprehensive LLM tools for prompt engineering
primary_use_case: Building and deploying AI agents to automate trading on Polymarket prediction markets.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, intelligent robot with a friendly demeanor is shown at a bustling marketplace. The robot is holding a digital tablet displaying fluctuating market graphs and news headlines, representing data sourcing. It's actively placing trades on a virtual Polymarket exchange interface projected in front of it, symbolizing autonomous trading. Subtle glowing lines connect the robot to various data feeds (news tickers, betting odds displays) and the Polymarket interface. The background features abstract representations of prediction markets as interconnected nodes. The overall style is a clean, vibrant 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clarity and a futuristic yet approachable aesthetic, with the repository name 'Polymarket Agents' subtly integrated into a digital sign.
